results.caption:
They didn't just build ransomware. They built a brand.

DragonForce didn't emerge from a basement — it launched like a startup. Logo. Affiliate portal. Revenue splits. A 80/20 deal sheet that would make a VC proud.

363 victims. 30+ countries. £300M+ in damage.

This is what happens when cybercrime goes corporate.

#DragonForce #Ransomware #CyberSecurity #RaaS #ThreatIntelligence
results.hook: A mythologized criminal brand rendered as dark corporate identity art stops the scroll and forces the question: who built this thing?

results.topic: The Binary Signature — Ransom Note Decoded
results.caption:
It arrives as a .txt file.
It reads like a contract.

DragonForce ransom notes are surgical — victim ID, decryption portal, deadline, price. No theatrics. Just business.

ChaCha8 encryption across Windows, Linux, and ESXi. BYOVD drivers killing your EDR before you even know you're hit.

By the time you read the note, it's already too late.

#DragonForce #RansomNote #IncidentResponse #ChaCha8 #CyberThreat
results.hook: A ransom note examined under forensic light like a murder weapon forces viewers to confront the cold operational reality of ransomware as a business transaction.

results.caption:
Malaysia? Russia? Hacktivists turned criminals?

DragonForce's origins remain one of cybersecurity's most debated questions. Early campaigns targeted Israeli infrastructure. Then the pivot. Then BreachForums. Then a cartel.

Attribution is hard. That's by design.

When your threat actor doesn't want to be found, your entire defensive posture changes.

#ThreatAttribution #DragonForce #CyberIntelligence #OSINT #NationState
results.hook: A shadowy map with competing attribution arrows and question marks captures the intelligence community's fundamental frustration with attributing DragonForce — and triggers fierce debate in the comments.

results.caption:
December 6, 2023. BreachForums.

A new thread appears. A new ransomware brand introduces itself to the world's most dangerous cybercrime marketplace.

DragonForce is open for business.

Affiliates welcome. Infrastructure ready. Targets already queued.

363 victims and £300M+ in damage would follow.

It started with a single post.

#BreachForums #DarkWeb #DragonForce #ZeroDay #CyberCrime
results.hook: The exact moment a new criminal enterprise goes public — a first post on BreachForums frozen in time like a crime scene photo — creates visceral dread and shareability.

results.caption:
March 19, 2025.

DragonForce didn't just announce an upgrade. They announced a cartel.

A new model. A new brand umbrella. Affiliates could now operate under their own names, using DragonForce infrastructure. Then came the absorption of RansomHub on April 8.

212.5% attack surge. 35 organizations hit in December alone.

This is what cybercrime's Series B looks like.

#DragonForceCartel #RansomHub #RaaS #CyberCrime #ThreatActor
results.hook: A press-conference aesthetic applied to a criminal cartel announcement subverts corporate launch-day imagery to create darkly compelling commentary on cybercrime's corporatization.

results.caption:
DragonForce doesn't just run ransomware. It runs a franchise.

Affiliates keep 80% of every ransom paid. The cartel takes 20% — and provides everything: encryption tools, negotiation portals, victim leak sites, and operational support.

This isn't hacking. It's a business model.

Announced March 19, 2025. Already absorbed RansomHub by April 8, 2025.

The cartel is growing. Are your defenses keeping up?

#DragonForce #RansomwareAsAService #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #RaaS
results.hook: The franchise model of cybercrime exposes how ransomware cartels operate like legitimate businesses — with revenue splits, affiliate programs, and branded toolkits.

results.caption:
363 confirmed victims. 30+ countries. A 212.5% surge in attacks.

In December 2025 alone — 35 organizations hit in a single month.

DragonForce isn't slowing down. It's accelerating.

Every data point on that wall was a real company. Real employees. Real consequences.

Know the scale. Prepare accordingly.

#DragonForce #Ransomware #CyberThreat #IncidentResponse #CyberAwareness
results.hook: A 212.5% attack surge visualized as a wall of falling victim names creates visceral urgency and forces audiences to confront the scale of this threat.

results.caption:
DragonForce doesn't have borders.

30+ countries. 363 victims. United States, United Kingdom, Australia — and dozens more.

The cartel launched December 6, 2023. By Summer 2025, it had hit Marks & Spencer, Co-op, and Harrods — causing £300M+ in damage to British retail alone.

This isn't a regional threat. It's a global campaign.

Geography is not a defense.

#DragonForce #GlobalCybersecurity #RansomwareAttack #CyberThreatIntelligence #InfoSec
results.hook: A fractured world map bleeding red across 30+ nations makes the geographic scale of DragonForce undeniable and personally relevant to international audiences.

results.caption:
Your EDR didn't get bypassed. It was murdered.

DragonForce uses BYOVD — Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver — loading truesight.sys and rentdrv2.sys to kill endpoint detection at the kernel level.

By the time the screen goes dark, it's already too late.

MITRE ATT&CK T1562.001 — Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools.

Kernel-level blind spots are ransomware's best friend.

#BYOVD #DragonForce #EndpointSecurity #MITREATTaCK #CyberDefense
results.hook: The moment your endpoint detection goes dark is visualized as a literal flatline — the most visceral way to communicate the terror of BYOVD attacks to security professionals.

results.caption:
No exploit. No zero-day. Just a phone call.

DragonForce collaborated with Scattered Spider — the group known for vishing, SIM swapping, and social engineering IT helpdesks into handing over credentials.

They don't need to break your firewall if they can convince your helpdesk to open the door.

Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods. All Summer 2025.

The most dangerous attack vector isn't in your tech stack. It's in your phone system.

#ScatteredSpider #SocialEngineering #DragonForce #Vishing #HumanRisk
results.hook: A smiling social engineer on a phone call next to a burning server rack destroys the myth that sophisticated attacks require technical exploits — the most dangerous tool is a human voice.

results.caption:
9 days. 9 days they were inside before anyone noticed.

DragonForce affiliates don't kick down the front door — they walk quietly through the halls, room by room, mapping every server, every credential, every backup.

This is called lateral movement. And most organizations only discover it when the encryption wave hits.

MITRE ATT&CK T1021 — Remote Services. T1078 — Valid Accounts. T1570 — Lateral Tool Transfer.

By Day 3 they had domain admin. By Day 6 they'd found the backups. By Day 9, they were ready.

How long would they survive undetected in your environment?

#DragonForce #Ransomware #LateralMovement #CyberSecurity #ZeroTrust
results.hook: The image visualizes the invisible horror of an attacker living inside your network for 9 days, prompting security professionals to audit their own dwell time.

results.caption:
They didn't just encrypt your data.

First, they took it.

6 terabytes. Quietly. Over days. Customer records. Financial reports. Executive emails. Intellectual property. Source code.

This is double extortion — DragonForce's signature. Encrypt AND exfiltrate. If you restore from backups, they still own you. They still publish.

The leak site is already built. Your company name is already in a folder.

MITRE ATT&CK T1041 — Exfiltration Over C2 Channel. T1567 — Exfiltration Over Web Service.

Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods. £300M+ impact. Summer 2025. Real companies. Real data. Real consequences.

Data loss prevention isn't optional anymore.

#DragonForce #DataExfiltration #DoubleExtortion #RansomwareAsAService #CyberResilience
results.hook: A number — 6TB — visualized as a physical hemorrhage of data transforms an abstract statistic into visceral organizational loss that security leaders cannot ignore.

results.caption:
Your backups were your last line of defense.

DragonForce knew that.

They spent days 1 through 6 mapping your backup infrastructure. By Day 7, they deleted it. Methodically. Completely. Volume Shadow Copies. Tape backups. Cloud snapshots. NAS replication.

All of it. Gone.

MITRE ATT&CK T1490 — Inhibit System Recovery. T1485 — Data Destruction.

When the encryption wave hit on Day 9, there was nothing to recover from.

The ransom note wasn't a question. It was a statement.

Air-gapped, immutable, offsite — your backup strategy needs all three. Today.

#DragonForce #BackupDestruction #IncidentResponse #RansomwareDefense #CyberSecurity
results.hook: The moment backups are destroyed is the moment an organization loses all negotiating power — visualizing this as a physical act of destruction triggers an immediate emotional response in IT professionals.

results.caption:
Then came the wave.

ChaCha8. Cross-platform. Windows. Linux. ESXi hypervisors.

Every file. Every VM. Every database. Simultaneously.

DragonForce doesn't encrypt one machine at a time. Affiliates deploy across the entire environment at once — worm-style propagation across every system they spent 9 days mapping.

The extension appears: .dragonforce_encrypted

The ransom note drops: DRAGON_RANSOM_NOTE.txt

36 seconds. That's how long it takes ChaCha8 to encrypt a 10GB file share.

363 confirmed victims. 30+ countries. 212.5% surge in attacks.

Dec 6, 2023 → BreachForums debut. The cartel is open for business.

#ChaCha8 #DragonForce #RansomwareEncryption #ESXi #CyberAttack
results.hook: A wave of encryption visualized as a physical force sweeping across a network operations center creates an almost cinematic disaster-movie moment that stops the scroll instantly.

results.caption:
Then the phone rings.

It's not automated. It's a person. A DragonForce affiliate trained in social engineering — the same tactics used with Scattered Spider to vish their way into your network in the first place.

They know your name. Your CFO's name. Your revenue. Your insurance policy limit.

'We have 6.2 terabytes of your data. We have your customer records. We know about the GDPR violations in your Q2 audit. You have 72 hours.'

Affiliates keep 80%. The cartel takes 20%. This is industrialized extortion.

Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods. They all got this call.

£300 million in losses. Summer 2025. Real boardrooms. Real decisions.

What would you say?

#DragonForce #RansomwareNegotiation #ScatteredSpider #CyberExtortion #IncidentResponse
results.hook: A ringing phone in a devastated executive office — with a ransom demand on screen — is the most human and therefore most terrifying moment in any ransomware attack, making this deeply personal for every C-suite viewer.

results.caption:
On April 8, 2025, DragonForce didn't just grow — it consumed.

RansomHub, one of the most active ransomware operations on the planet, was absorbed into the DragonForce cartel in a single announcement. Overnight, DragonForce inherited affiliates, infrastructure, victim pipelines, and operational muscle.

This is what consolidation in the cybercrime economy looks like.

One cartel. One brand. Exponentially more reach.

If you thought tracking one RaaS group was hard before April 8th, 2025 — you now have a much bigger problem.

#Ransomware #DragonForce #RansomHub #CyberThreatIntelligence #InfoSec
results.hook: The visual of one criminal empire swallowing another overnight makes the threat feel unstoppable and sparks immediate fear-driven shares among security leaders.

results.caption:
Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods.

Three of Britain's most recognisable retail brands. All hit within weeks of each other. Summer 2025.

The financial damage? Conservative estimates put it at £300 million and climbing. M&S alone suspended online orders, lost an estimated £60M+ per week, and watched their share price fall sharply.

This wasn't a coordinated nation-state attack. This was a ransomware cartel operating with commercial efficiency — DragonForce and its affiliates, with Scattered Spider providing the social engineering access.

Your brand recognition is not a shield. It's a targeting reticle.

#DragonForce #Ransomware #UKCyberSecurity #RetailCybersecurity #ScatteredSpider
results.hook: The sight of iconic British retail brands visually shattered by a cyberattack makes the abstract digital threat viscerally real to every business professional scrolling LinkedIn.

results.topic: Full MITRE Kill Chain — All 10 Steps
results.caption:
Here's exactly how DragonForce moves through your network — mapped to the MITRE ATT&CK framework.

This isn't theoretical. This is the documented kill chain observed across 363+ confirmed victims.

Step 1: Phishing / Vishing (with Scattered Spider)
Step 2: Valid Accounts — stolen credentials
Step 3: BYOVD — truesight.sys / rentdrv2.sys to kill EDR
Step 4: Discovery — network and system enumeration
Step 5: Lateral Movement — living off the land
Step 6: Credential Dumping — LSASS access
Step 7: Data Exfiltration — double extortion leverage
Step 8: ChaCha8 Encryption — Windows, Linux, ESXi
Step 9: Ransom Note Deployment
Step 10: Extortion Portal — 80/20 affiliate payout

Every step is a decision point where defenders can intervene. Are you monitoring all 10?

#MITRE #ATTACKFramework #DragonForce #ThreatHunting #IncidentResponse
results.hook: Security professionals will save and share a visually dramatic step-by-step MITRE kill chain because it's both a learning tool and a piece of striking art they can reference.

results.caption:
DragonForce has 10 attack steps. You have 5 critical controls that break the chain.

Here's where the leverage is:

🔒 DEFENSE 1: MFA + Phishing-Resistant Auth — kills Initial Access at Step 1
🔒 DEFENSE 2: Kernel Driver Allowlisting — stops BYOVD dead at Step 3
🔒 DEFENSE 3: EDR Tamper Protection + Canary Drivers — detects truesight.sys before execution
🔒 DEFENSE 4: Network Segmentation + Lateral Movement Detection — collapses Steps 4 & 5
🔒 DEFENSE 5: Immutable Offsite Backups + Recovery Rehearsal — eliminates ransomware leverage entirely

None of these are exotic. All of them are available today. Most organizations have at least one gap.

Which one is yours?

#CyberDefense #ZeroTrust #DragonForce #RansomwarePrevention #SecurityControls
results.hook: A visually dramatic 'shield vs dragon' defense framework gives security professionals an immediately shareable tactical reference that positions them as knowledgeable by sharing it.

results.topic: The Dragon Is Already Inside The Walls
results.caption:
The perimeter is a myth.

DragonForce doesn't break down your front door. It walks through it — using your own credentials, your own tools, your own trusted processes.

By the time ChaCha8 encryption fires, the dragon has been inside your walls for 72-96 hours. It has your data. It knows your backup locations. It has mapped your crown jewels.

The ransom demand isn't the attack. The ransom demand is the invoice.

The attack happened days ago.

363 organizations learned this lesson the hard way. In 30 countries. Across every sector.

You don't need better walls. You need to hunt what's already inside them.

— DragonForce: Active since December 2023. 363 victims. Still recruiting affiliates.

#DragonForce #ThreatHunting #Ransomware #ZeroTrust #CyberResilience
results.hook: The haunting cinematic reveal of a threat already inside the network — not at the perimeter — creates the visceral dread that makes senior executives and board members stop scrolling.
